description_long: This object is a vintage-style gramophone record player featuring a large, flared brass horn with a polygonal rim, which serves as the sound amplifier. The horn is attached to a wooden base that houses the mechanical components and provides a sturdy foundation. On top of the base sits a circular turntable platter, bordered by a green ring, designed to hold vinyl records. A metallic tonearm extends from the side, curving over the platter, and is equipped with a small stylus at its end for reading the grooves of a record. The base is finished in a dark, wood-grain texture, and a winding handle is visible on one side, suggesting manual operation. The overall design is ornate and classic, evoking early 20th-century audio equipment.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large brass horn, wooden base, and a visible tonearm and turntable platter. The design is ornate and classic, with a dark wood finish and metallic accents.
description_view_0: The front view highlights the wide, flared opening of the brass horn, with the wooden base and turntable mostly obscured.
description_view_1: This side view shows the full length of the horn, the circular turntable platter, the tonearm, and the winding handle on the right side of the wooden base.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the back of the horn, the tonearm extending over the platter, and the winding handle protruding from the base.
description_view_3: The opposite side view reveals the horn's profile, the platter, and the tonearm, with the winding handle less visible.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, circular, and sits atop the wooden base. It is black with a green outer ring, appears to be made of metal or hard plastic, and is proportionally smaller than the horn. The surface is smooth, with no visible grooves or patterns.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, metallic, and curves from the side of the base over the platter. It is medium in length, silver in color, and has a smooth, glossy finish. The tonearm is positioned above the platter, suggesting it is ready to play a record.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is present, attached to the end of the tonearm. It is small, metallic, and appears as a distinct component at the tip of the tonearm.
